key.name: db-path
key.description: Absolute path to the last used database

key.name: db-list
key.description: List of known databases as (display_name, absolute_path) tuples

key.name: show-next-otp
key.description: If enabled, when the current OTP expires, the next one is shown too

key.name: notification-enabled
key.description: If enabled, a notification will pop-up everytime an OTP is copied

key.name: search-column
key.description: Select whether searching will be done on the Account or Issuer column

key.name: window-width
key.description: Default window width

key.name: window-height
key.description: Default window height

key.name: dark-theme
key.description: Whether the application should use a dark theme.

key.name: auto-lock
key.description: Whether to auto lock the application or not

key.name: auto-lock-timeout
key.description: Lock the application on idle after the specified amount of minutes

key.name: secret-service
key.description: Whether to store the database password in the system keyring so the app unlocks automatically after login

key.name: search-provider-enabled
key.description: Whether the D-Bus search provider (GNOME Shell / KDE KRunner) is enabled

key.name: show-validity-seconds
key.description: Show the remaining seconds of validity for each TOTP token

key.name: validity-color
key.description: Color used for the token validity countdown

key.name: validity-warning-color
key.description: Color used for the token validity countdown when time is running low

key.name: show-sidebar
key.description: Whether the database sidebar is visible

key.name: clipboard-clear-timeout
key.description: Seconds after which the clipboard is automatically cleared after copying an OTP code. Set to 0 to disable.

key.name: minimize-to-tray
key.description: Minimize window to system tray instead of closing

key.name: last-seen-version
key.description: Tracks the last version the user has seen, used to show What's New dialogs on upgrade

key.name: last-export-time
key.description: Unix timestamp of the most recent successful export, used to remind the user to take periodic backups. 0 means never exported.
